title: Bridge Essay: Missing images: autobiographical memory in Aphantasia and blindness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imagination as real perception — shared neural circuitry for imagery and perception, hallucination as continuum.

**Missing images: autobiographical memory in Aphantasia and blindness.**. Frontiers in cognition.. PMID: 42339253. DOI: 10.3389/fcogn.2025.1644533. Mental visual imagery, especially the ability to construct naturalistic scenes seems central to vivid episodic autobiographical memory (AM). This mini review will first highlight the neural anatomy of different aspects of mental imagery, focusing on the roles of the hippocampus, ventromedial prefrontal cortex and posterior neocortex and the consequences of damage to these regions to AM.
